title: Kdo to je? and Jak se jmenujete?
summary: Ask who someone is, ask a person’s name politely, and hear several authentic ways of answering.
objectives: Recognize Jmenuji se, Jmenuju se, and Já jsem as natural answers

sections.heading: Who is this?
sections.body: Use kdo for a person and co for a thing. Kdo to je? asks who a person is. Kdo je tohle? points to someone close by.
examples: Kdo to je? — To je Václav Havel.
Kdo je tohle? — To je Petra.

sections.heading: What is your name?
sections.body: Jak se jmenujete? is the polite or plural question. Spoken answers commonly use Jmenuju se…, while Jmenuji se… is a more formal-style variant. Já jsem… is also natural.
examples: Jak se jmenujete? — Jmenuju se Lenka.

resources.title: Jak se jmenujete?
resources.kind: Reality Czech interview video and annotations
resources.note: Unscripted answers from multiple Czech speakers.

exercises.explanation: Jak se jmenujete? is the polite or plural form.

exercises.prompt: Which question asks about a person rather than a thing?
Give a natural Czech answer meaning “My name is Lenka.”
exercises.explanation: All three forms occur naturally in the Reality Czech interview.
